title: After Action Review — Landing Page Modes & Performance Fix
scope: Fix broken landing page mode cards and improve page load performance on the Viperball NiceGUI app. 9 files changed, +117 / -50 lines

rationale.item: Tab rendering used hardcoded mode keys that didn't match the tab panel names, so clicking "College Season", "Quick Game", etc. from the landing page opened blank panels.
Four heavy page modules (game_simulator, season_simulator, dynasty_mode, dq_mode) were imported at module level, forcing all their transitive dependencies (pandas, plotly, engine) to load at startup.

actions.description: Fixed tab rendering hardcoded mode keys mismatch; rewrote _render_tab() to use tab_panel context correctly (it previously loaded content into detached containers inside a timer callback without proper NiceGUI context).
Cached load_teams_from_directory()/load_team_identity() results in shared dict instead of reading every team JSON file from disk on every render.
Cached read_conferences_from_team_files() result in shared dict.
Moved four heavy page module imports (game_simulator, season_simulator, dynasty_mode, dq_mode) to lazy imports inside tab render functions.
Added isLooping() guards and frame-rate throttling to P5.js sketches that started immediately and ran continuously even when not visible.
Fixed play_tab forwarding: initial sync render lambda changed from `lambda n: _switch_to(n)` to `lambda n, **kw: _switch_to(n, **kw)` so mode cards can direct to specific sub-tabs.
Moved pandas and plotly imports to lazy imports inside the functions that use them.
Commits: d78bdf1 "Fix landing page mode cards and improve load performance"; 1b36b6e "Improve performance: lazy imports, caching, and fix play_tab forwarding".

validation_claims.claim: Mode card navigation should now correctly open the corresponding play tab
validation_claims.check_method: Run the NiceGUI app and click each landing page mode card, confirming the matching play tab opens.

validation_claims.claim: Landing page should render faster due to deferred module loading and cached disk I/O
validation_claims.check_method: Profile startup/first-paint time before and after the commits.

validation_claims.claim: P5.js animations should consume less CPU when idle
validation_claims.check_method: Observe browser CPU usage with the page idle and verify isLooping guards in the sketches.

non_actions.item: _simulate() sync handler in game_simulator.py (load_team() is blocking I/O)
non_actions.reason: user-triggered, not on load path (severity Low)
Unavoidable — home page needs team metadata before first paint (severity Low)
Two different data shapes; could benefit from a docstring (severity Trivial)

section_headers_original: After Action Review — Landing Page Modes & Performance Fix
parser_notes: Testing Notes are expectations ("should") rather than measured results; recorded as validation claims with evidence_cited none. Date from header metadata (**Date:** 2026-03-07).
